req.title: Manager updates team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates the manager team update feature across three critical dimensions: (1) positive case confirms valid updates succeed with audit trail, (2) negative cases verify authentication/authorization and validation barriers are enforced, (3) boundary cases test field constraints at limits. Tests are designed to be independent, deterministic, and traceable to ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4 techniques. All tests assume a running hr-app instance at BASE_URL with seeded test data.

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_valid_all_fields
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid session token
actions: POST /teams/team-001 with valid update payload
Payload includes: name='Engineering Core', description='Main eng team', max_size=50
Request includes Authorization header with manager session token
expected: Response body contains updated name='Engineering Core'
Audit record written with action='TEAM_UPDATE', actor='manager_id', resource='team-001'
Team record in database reflects new values
trace: R-WEB-010: expected outcome 'response 200 or 201 with entity id'
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_manager_updates_team_valid_all_fields():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    auth_token = '<get_manager_token>'
    team_id = 'team-001'
    
    payload = {
        'name': 'Engineering Core',
        'description': 'Main eng team',
        'max_size': 50
    }
    
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert data['team_id'] == team_id
    assert data['name'] == 'Engineering Core'
    assert data['description'] == 'Main eng team'
    
    audit = <verify_audit_record>(team_id, 'TEAM_UPDATE')
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit['actor_role'] == 'manager'

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid session token
actions: POST /teams/team-002 with incomplete payload
Payload omits mandatory field 'name'
Payload includes: description='Test team', max_size=25
expected: Response body contains field_errors with 'name' listed as missing
trace: R-WEB-010: error pathway '400 on validation error'
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_updates_team_missing_mandatory_field():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    auth_token = '<get_manager_token>'
    team_id = 'team-002'
    
    payload = {
        'description': 'Test team',
        'max_size': 25
    }
    
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 400
    data = response.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'VALIDATION_ERROR'
    assert 'name' in data['field_errors']
    
    audit = <verify_audit_record>(team_id, 'TEAM_UPDATE')
    assert audit is None

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_unauthenticated
actions: POST /teams/team-003 without Authorization header
Payload includes valid name='Updated Team'
Request omits or provides invalid bearer token
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_updates_team_unauthenticated():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    team_id = 'team-003'
    
    payload = {'name': 'Updated Team'}
    headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    data = response.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'UNAUTHORIZED'
    
    audit = <verify_audit_record>(team_id, 'TEAM_UPDATE')
    assert audit is None

tests.name: test_non_manager_updates_team_forbidden
preconditions: User is authenticated with role='employee' (not manager)
actions: POST /teams/team-004 with valid update payload
Request includes Authorization header with employee session token
Payload includes name='New Name', description='New desc'
trace: R-WEB-010: error pathway '403 if role forbidden'
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_non_manager_updates_team_forbidden():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    auth_token = '<get_employee_token>'
    team_id = 'team-004'
    
    payload = {
        'name': 'New Name',
        'description': 'New desc'
    }
    
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403
    data = response.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'FORBIDDEN'
    
    audit = <verify_audit_record>(team_id, 'TEAM_UPDATE')
    assert audit is None

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_name_boundary_max_length
preconditions: Team name field has max_length=255 characters
actions: POST /teams/team-005 with name at exact max boundary
name field set to 255-character string
Payload includes valid description and max_size
expected: Team name persisted with full 255 characters
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory field validation with boundary constraint
BVA: upper boundary of name field length
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_updates_team_name_boundary_max_length():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    auth_token = '<get_manager_token>'
    team_id = 'team-005'
    long_name = 'A' * 255
    
    payload = {
        'name': long_name,
        'description': 'Test',
        'max_size': 50
    }
    
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert len(data['name']) == 255
    assert data['name'] == long_name
    
    audit = <verify_audit_record>(team_id, 'TEAM_UPDATE')
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_name_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: Team name field has max_length=255 characters
actions: POST /teams/team-006 with name exceeding max boundary
name field set to 256-character string (one over limit)
Payload includes valid description and max_size
expected: Error message indicates name exceeds maximum length
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory field validation with boundary constraint
BVA: beyond upper boundary of name field length

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_max_size_boundary_zero
preconditions: max_size field accepts positive integers, minimum=1
actions: POST /teams/team-007 with max_size at zero boundary
max_size set to 0 (below valid minimum)
Payload includes valid name and description
expected: Error message indicates max_size must be >= 1
trace: R-WEB-010: field validation at lower boundary
BVA: below minimum boundary of max_size field
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_manager_updates_team_max_size_boundary_zero():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    auth_token = '<get_manager_token>'
    team_id = 'team-007'
    
    payload = {
        'name': 'Team Name',
        'description': 'Team Desc',
        'max_size': 0
    }
    
    headers = {
        'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
    }
    
    response = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/teams/{team_id}',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 400
    data = response.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'VALIDATION_ERROR'
    assert 'max_size' in data['field_errors']
